name: The Altus Sopor
description: The ancient practice of deepening one’s focus on the Force through mastery of the body and breath. Altus Sopor encompasses the Art of Movement, Force Speed, and the meditative void of Emptiness — the foundational Control disciplines that allow a practitioner to channel the Force inward and become something more than their physical form allows.
fluff: Altus Sopor was the traditional term for the ability to increase a user’s focus on the Force. It incorporated alchaka, breath control, Force speed, and the meditative void — the foundation of every Jedi’s control training. Masters who perfected the sopor could merge so deeply into the Force that they became nearly invisible to others. You have begun walking that path.

abilities.name: Art of Movement
abilities.rule: You may use Control (Grit) in place of Evasion (Reflex) for all Dodge defense rolls — you evade through Force-guided movement: dodging traps, debris, hazards, and attacks with preternatural grace. You gain narrative permission for wall-running, Force-assisted leaps, and vertical traversal through the Force. This ability is DISABLED while you are affected by [Rattled], [Disoriented], or any condition that disrupts mental focus.
abilities.shorthand: Use Control (Grit) for Dodge. No Openings from movement while Force-guided.

abilities.name: Enhance Attribute
abilities.rule: Diceless Maneuver. Spend 1 Edge + your Maneuver pip. Choose one arena: Physique or Reflex. That arena gains +1 Effect Tier on all rolls. Duration: Tactical. At the start of each subsequent turn, you may spend 1 Edge to sustain the enhancement — no action cost. You may only enhance one arena at a time. Switching arenas requires a new activation.

abilities.name: Force Speed
abilities.rule: Channel the Force into explosive velocity. Roll Control (Grit). The result generates Move Points that must be spent on your Move action this turn (1 point = 1 zone). Movement fueled by Force Speed presents NO Openings, even when leaving Engaged zones. Unspent points are lost at the end of your turn. Force Speed movement fuels Art of Movement traversal (wall-running, vertical movement).
abilities.risk: Failure wastes the maneuver — the Force does not answer your call for speed this round.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: 2 Move Points.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: 4 Move Points.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: 6 Move Points.

abilities.name: Flowing Counter
abilities.rule: When you Dodge against an Engaged attacker and achieve a Masterful Success (Control 8+), instead of the standard +1 Tier bonus, the attacker presents an Opening to you which you may immediately Exploit (costs your Exploit pip). Your evasion flows seamlessly into a counterattack position.

abilities.name: Emptiness
abilities.rule: Short Rest only. Roll Control (Grit). The current Toll count on the Destiny tracker sets the static difficulty. While meditating (narrative truth, always active during the rest): you are undetectable by Force senses, you clear 1 mental condition ([Rattled] or [Disoriented]), and you are physically vulnerable and interruptible. To carry effects into the next scene, spend 1 Edge.
abilities.risk: On failure, the Edge spent to carry effects forward is wasted — the meditation did not reach sufficient depth.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Force detection against you is [Weakened] until the end of the next scene.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting. Additionally, you are immune to [Rattled] and [Disoriented] until the end of the next scene.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful. Additionally, gain +1 Effect Tier on all Control (Grit) rolls until the end of the next scene.

name: The Pathfinder
description: The Pathfinder does not conquer the wilderness — she reads it. Where others see hostile terrain, she sees shelter, sustenance, and safe passage. She moves through fungal jungles and toxic wastes the way a dockworker moves through a spaceport: with the casual confidence of someone who has been here before, even when she has not. The galaxy is full of planets that want to kill you. The Pathfinder is the reason your squad walks out the other side.
fluff: Every expedition needs someone who can read the ground. Not with sensors or satellite maps, but with instinct earned from years of sleeping in places where the air can kill you and the water fights back. The Pathfinder is the one who stops the column before the sinkhole, identifies the spore bloom before the first sneeze, and somehow produces a medpac from a pile of moss and tree bark. On Felucia, the clone troopers had their training and their armor. It was the scouts who kept them alive — the ones who knew which fungi were footholds and which were digestive tracts. In the Outer Rim, when the nav computer fails and the terrain stretches past the edge of the map, you follow the Pathfinder. She has never needed a map.

abilities.name: Wilderness Awareness
abilities.rule:
In wilderness or natural terrain, you may use Survival (Grit) in place of Tactics (Wits) when rolling Join Battle. You read terrain the way a tactician reads a battlefield — sight lines, choke points, elevation, and escape routes are instinctive.

Additionally, you may use Survival (Grit) in place of Medicine (Wits) when treating natural wounds, poisons, diseases, or environmental injuries — even with scant or improvised supplies. You know which root draws out venom, which bark reduces fever, and which moss stops bleeding. A proper medpac helps, but you do not need one.
abilities.shorthand: Survival replaces Tactics for Join Battle in wilderness. Survival replaces Medicine for natural wounds/poisons/diseases, even with scant supplies.

abilities.name: Scrounge
abilities.rule: During a Long Rest in wilderness or natural terrain, you may spend a maneuver to scavenge your surroundings. Roll Survival (Grit). Based on your result tier, choose one item from the corresponding menu. You choose one item from the highest tier you qualify for. Items scrounged are improvised equivalents — they function identically but look like they were assembled from bark, sap, and whatever was in your pockets.
abilities.risk: On failure, you find nothing useful — the terrain has been picked clean, or the local flora is simply inedible.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Choose one: Field Rations (10cr equivalent), Glow Rod (25cr equivalent), or Syntherope (10cr equivalent).

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Choose one: Bacta Patch (20cr equivalent), Stimulant Tabs (20cr equivalent), or Camouflaged Camp (concealed rest site — the party cannot be found by passive patrols during this rest).

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Choose one: Medpac (100cr equivalent) or Rare Find (GM determines — a useful item, resource, or material native to the environment that would not normally be available without a market).

abilities.shorthand: Long Rest maneuver: roll Survival (Grit). Choose one item from a tier-based menu — rations/glow rod/rope at Fleeting, bacta patch/stimulant tabs/camo camp at Masterful, medpac/rare find at Legendary.

abilities.name: Vanguard
abilities.rule:
When you take the Assess action in wilderness or natural terrain, you may declare that you are scouting along your proposed path of travel. Your questions are scoped to the route ahead: what dangers lie along this path, what terrain changes are coming, whether the path is passable, and what lives or operates in the area you are heading toward.

This uses Survival (Grit) for the Assess roll. On success, you gain your questions as normal. On failure, you gain no information, and the GM may trigger an encounter as a consequence of your scouting — you pushed too far ahead and something found you first.
abilities.shorthand: Assess in wilderness using Survival (Grit), scoped to scouting the route ahead. Failure = no info + GM may trigger encounter.

abilities.name: Instinct
abilities.rule:
When an ally would be attacked or affected by an unknown, unidentified hazard in natural terrain, you may spend 1 Edge to immediately assume the risk. You take the hit instead of your ally and resist the hazard with Survival (Grit). This roll is [Optimized] — your Control die steps up.

You saw it before they did. The discolored ground, the shift in the air, the way the canopy went quiet. You shoved them aside and took the hit because you knew exactly what was coming — and more importantly, you knew exactly how to take it.
abilities.shorthand: Spend 1 Edge when ally faces unknown natural hazard. You assume the risk and resist with Survival (Grit), [Optimized].

abilities.name: Native Ground
abilities.rule:
When you Assess using Survival in natural terrain and score 8+ on your Control die, you replenish 1 Edge. Your mastery of the wild is self-sustaining — the more you read the terrain, the more confident you become, and confidence is currency.

Additionally, once per session, you may spend Edge to retroactively declare that you prepared for a specific environmental contingency. "I cached water at the ridge two hours ago." "I marked this trail on the way in." "I set a perimeter alarm last night." "I brought thermal wraps — I have been to ice planets before." The GM determines whether the preparation is plausible given the environment, your travel time, and available materials, but cannot deny it simply because you did not announce it earlier. You are always preparing. The party just does not notice until it matters.
abilities.shorthand: Assess with Survival in natural terrain, 8+ Control = replenish 1 Edge. Once per session, spend Edge to retroactively declare environmental preparation.

name: The Shii-Cho Knight
description: Form I — The Way of the Sarlacc. The oldest lightsaber discipline, and the one every Jedi learns first. Shii-Cho is not elegant. It is not subtle. It is the foundation — wide sweeping arcs that trace the six zones of attack, powered by determination rather than finesse. Where other forms specialize, Shii-Cho endures. Where other forms seek advantage, Shii-Cho seeks resolution. The blade does not stop moving until the fight is over — or until the Jedi decides it is.
fluff: Every form that came after was built on this one. When the duelist's precision fails, when the guardian's wall breaks, when the aggressor's fury burns out — what remains is Shii-Cho. The first thing you learned. The last thing still standing.

abilities.name: The Determination Form
abilities.rule:
When you attack with a Lightsaber or a Light or Medium bladed melee weapon, you may roll Melee (Grit) instead of Melee (Physique). Power comes from determination, not muscle. The Force disciplines the body — every strike lands with the certainty of absolute resolve.

Sun Djem: When your melee attack with a lightsaber would reduce a target to 0 Vitality, you may declare Sun Djem. The target is left at 1 Vitality and rendered Helpless. You determine whether they fall unconscious or immediately exit the scene — alive, but injured. The fight is over. You chose to let them walk away from it.
abilities.shorthand: Melee (Grit) replaces Melee (Physique) with lightsaber or light/medium bladed melee. Sun Djem: on reducing target to 0 Vitality, may leave at 1 Vitality + Helpless instead.

abilities.name: Sarlacc Sweep
abilities.rule: When you are Engaged with two or more enemies and your melee attack reduces a target to 0 Vitality, or anytime you declare Sun Djem on a target, the next Engaged enemy presents an Opening. You may Exploit this Opening to make a new melee attack against them. If this attack also reduces its target to 0 Vitality or you declare Sun Djem, the chain continues — the next Engaged enemy presents another Opening. The sweep continues as long as there are Engaged enemies, targets keep falling, and you have Exploit pips to spend.
abilities.shorthand: On kill or Sun Djem while Engaged with 2+: next Engaged enemy presents Opening. Exploit to attack. Chain continues on each kill/Sun Djem.

abilities.name: Disarming Slash
abilities.rule: After resolving a melee attack with a lightsaber, you may activate this gambit. Increase your effect tier by 1 — if this would reduce the target to 0 Vitality, you may declare Sun Djem. The target is left at 1 Vitality and rendered Helpless. If the increased tier would not reduce them to 0 Vitality, deal damage at your original effect tier minus 1 instead. The reach for mercy costs you when it falls short.
abilities.shorthand: Gambit: +1 tier check. If would reduce to 0 Vitality, declare Sun Djem (1 Vitality, Helpless). If not, deal original tier minus 1.

abilities.name: Inner Peace
abilities.rule: At the start of any action or reaction, you may spend 1 Edge to clear one of the following conditions affecting you: Disoriented, Rattled, Staggered, or one instance of Weakened (such as Weakened Attacks, Weakened Defenses, or Weakened to a specific arena). The oldest form teaches that the blade begins in the mind — when the mind is clear, the blade follows.
abilities.shorthand: Spend 1 Edge at start of action/reaction to clear Disoriented, Rattled, Staggered, or one Weakened instance.

abilities.name: The Foundation
abilities.rule: You have mastered the foundation. All lightsaber forms descend from Form I — and now you understand this truth completely. You gain the Tier 1 ability of every other lightsaber form vocation. These are not borrowed techniques. They were always here, inside the oldest form, waiting to be recognized.
abilities.shorthand: Gain the T1 ability of every other lightsaber form vocation.

name: The Duelist
description: Precision, timing, and a blade held with intent. The Duelist is a trained close-combat specialist who controls the fight through footwork, measure, and reading their opponent — parrying, waiting, and striking when the geometry is right. Whether the weapon is a vibroblade or a lightsaber, the discipline is the same.
fluff: Makashi was a lightsaber form built for dueling other saber-wielders — precise, economical, and contemptuous of brute strength. Count Dooku used it to dismantle two Jedi at once without breaking a sweat. But the same principles show up in fencing salons on Coruscant, vibroblade pits on Nar Shaddaa, and back-alley knife fights on Corellia. A Kel Dor bodyguard with a force pike. A Mandalorian with a beskad. A dock worker who took three months of lessons and kept the blade. The weapon doesn't make the duelist. The hours do.

abilities.name: En Garde
abilities.rule: You may Endure melee attacks using Melee (Physique) in place of Endure. When you do, treat your armor rating as Light for the Physique step calculation — even if you are unarmored, your blade compensates. You may draw or ready a Melee weapon as part of any Attack action at no Maneuver cost.
abilities.shorthand: Endure melee attacks using Melee (Physique). Armor treated as Light. Draw Melee free on Attack.

abilities.name: Read the Blade
abilities.rule: After resolving an Endure defense using Melee (via En Garde), spend 1 tier. You gain [Optimized] on your next Melee weapon attack against that attacker before the end of your next turn. You read their technique in the exchange and positioned yourself for the counter.

abilities.name: Press the Advantage
abilities.rule: Melee (Physique) vs target's Resolve. Drive forward with controlled aggression — precise bladework that tests your opponent's nerve and forces them to give ground. Risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you overextended and they saw it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Target presents an Opening to you.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled] and [Exposed]. Target presents an Opening to you.

abilities.name: Riposte
abilities.rule: When you defend using Melee (via En Garde) and score 8+ on your Control die, you may forgo the standard +1 Effect tier. Instead, the attacker immediately presents an Opening which you may Exploit to make an immediate Melee attack against them.

abilities.name: Measure of a Man
abilities.rule: Use your Maneuver to Assess an Engaged opponent using Melee (Wits) — reading their guard, footwork, and tells. You gain the normal Assess questions from the GM (as if using Tactics). Additionally, you may spend 1 Edge. If you do, your next Melee weapon attack against that target this round gains [Optimized] and [Empowered]. You've already won. They just don't know it yet.

name: The Juggernaut
description: The Juggernaut does not dodge. He does not flinch. He walks into the blast zone, shrugs off the hit, and keeps moving. Where others build around speed and reflexes, the Juggernaut builds around the armor on his back — not to overcome its limitations, but to lean into its strengths. The heavier the plate, the harder the hit, the more punishment he can absorb. He is not fast. He is not subtle. He is the reason they stopped shooting.
fluff: Every soldier learns to wear armor. Most learn to tolerate it. A few learn to depend on it. The Juggernaut is the professional who treats their armor the way a swordsman treats a blade — as an extension of the body, maintained, understood, and trusted absolutely. On the Outer Rim, when the blaster fire starts and the squad dives for cover, there is always one who stays standing. The enemy empties a power cell into his chest. He does not go down. He does not slow down. Eventually, the enemy stops shooting — not because they ran out of ammunition, but because they realized it was not working.

abilities.name: Broken In
abilities.rule:
While wearing armor, you suffer no penalties to your Control die (disciplines) due to encumbrance or bulk. You have trained to operate in your armor — the weight distribution is second nature and your fine motor control is unaffected. You still suffer any penalties to your Reflex arena, including the Evasion step-down for medium and heavy armor.

Additionally, when you choose to take Trauma to your Physique while wearing armor, you may soak 1 additional Vitality (3 instead of the normal 2).
abilities.shorthand: No Control die penalties from armor. Reflex penalties still apply. Physique Trauma soaks 3 Vitality instead of 2 while armored.

abilities.name: Durasteel Nerve
abilities.rule:
When you fail an Endure defense roll and spend Edge to reroll, you are [Optimized] for the reroll — your Control die steps up for that Endure roll. The first hit found a gap. The second one will not.

Your armor taught you something most soldiers never learn: the moment after you get hit is the moment you are most dangerous, because now you know exactly where you are vulnerable and you will not make that mistake twice.
abilities.shorthand: Failed Endure + Edge reroll = [Optimized] (Control die steps up for the reroll).

abilities.name: Plated Conditioning
abilities.rule:
Your conditioning while wearing armor is fully ingrained. When you choose to take Trauma to your Physique, the additional Vitality soaked now scales with your armor type:

- Light armor: +1 additional Vitality soaked (3 total)
- Medium armor: +2 additional Vitality soaked (4 total)
- Heavy armor: +3 additional Vitality soaked (5 total)

This replaces the flat +1 from Broken In — heavier armor now rewards your investment with greater damage absorption per Trauma.

Additionally, you do not suffer any penalties from exhaustion faster than someone not wearing armor. Extended marches, desert heat, sustained combat — the armor is not a factor in your fatigue.
abilities.shorthand: Physique Trauma soak scales with armor: light +1 (3), medium +2 (4), heavy +3 (5). No exhaustion penalties from wearing armor.

abilities.name: "Is That All?"
abilities.rule:
Gambit on Endure. When you successfully Endure an attack, you may spend 1 tier from your result to reduce the incoming effect by 1 tier and impose [Rattled] on the attacker. You took the hit clean and did not flinch — they expected you to go down and you did not. That shakes people.

[Rattled]: Mental debuff — the attacker's Control die steps down on mental actions. They are shaken, demoralized, and second-guessing whether hitting you again is worth the effort.
abilities.shorthand: Endure gambit: spend 1 tier → reduce incoming effect by 1 tier + attacker gains [Rattled].

abilities.name: Siege Engine
abilities.rule:
When you Endure an attack and score 8+ on your Control die, you may choose to immediately clear any mental condition affecting you instead of taking the normal +1 effect tier bonus. The hit snaps you back to focus — pain has a way of cutting through fear, confusion, and demoralization.

Additionally, your armor works for you even when you are not actively defending. When you are attacked by any attack you could Endure (regardless of whether you actually Endure or Dodge), your armor passively reduces the attacker's Power rating before you even roll:

- Light armor: Power -1
- Medium armor: Power -2
- Heavy armor: Power -3

This reduction applies before your defense roll. The enemy's weapon is simply less effective against you — the blast dissipates across the plate, the vibroblade skitters off a pauldron, the slug flattens against durasteel. They are not fighting you. They are fighting your armor. And your armor is winning.
abilities.shorthand: Endure 8+ Control: clear mental condition instead of +1 effect. Armor passively reduces attacker Power (light -1, medium -2, heavy -3) before rolling.

name: The Shockboxer
description: Fists, shock-gloves, and an iron chin. The Shockboxer is a disciplined fighter who builds momentum through combinations — each hit setting up the next until the opponent can't stand anymore. You don't need a blaster. You need them close enough to hit.
fluff: Shockboxing pits across the Outer Rim draw crowds from Nar Shaddaa to Ord Mantell. The fighters wear shock-gloves that amplify impact without cutting skin — the sport is about endurance, reading your opponent, and finding the knockout. The best boxers don't just win rounds. They end fights. You trained in those pits. You learned to take a hit, give two back, and stay standing when everyone else is on the floor.

abilities.name: Iron Chin
abilities.rule: You may use Brawl (Physique) in place of Endure for Defense rolls while Engaged. When you take Stun damage and remain conscious, gain [Optimized] on your next Brawl attack.
abilities.shorthand: Use Brawl (Physique) for Endure while Engaged. Stun damage → Optimized next Brawl.

abilities.name: Combo Counter
abilities.rule: Track Brawl hits that deal damage to the same target. On the 2nd hit: target gains [Rattled]. On the 3rd hit: target gains [Exposed]. Combo Counter resets at the end of your turn.
abilities.shorthand: Track Brawl hits on same target: 2nd → Rattled, 3rd → Exposed. Resets end of turn.

abilities.name: Body Work
abilities.rule: After resolving a Brawl attack that deals damage, spend 1 tier. This hit counts as 2 hits for your Combo Counter. A well-placed body shot fast-tracks the punishment.

abilities.name: Roll With It
abilities.rule: When you defend using Brawl (via Iron Chin), spend 1 tier from the defense result. The attacker immediately presents an Opening to you.

abilities.name: The Knockout
abilities.rule: When your Combo Counter reaches 3+ on a target and your Brawl attack resolves at Masterful or higher, you may declare a Knockout. The target is immediately [Incapacitated]. Once per scene.
abilities.shorthand: Combo 3+ and Masterful+ Brawl → target Incapacitated. 1/scene.

name: The Grifter
description: The Grifter doesn't fight fair because the Grifter doesn't fight at all — not if the job is done right. They lie for a living, and they're so good at it that the truth becomes whatever they say it is. Their power isn't in the moment — it's in the setup, the preparation, the long game that pays off three scenes after the seed was planted.
fluff: Hondo Ohnaka convinced both sides of a war he was on their side and walked away with both payments. Lando talked his way into Cloud City's administration and ran the place for years on charm and nerve. The best grifters don't need blasters — they need a mark, a story, and enough time to make both work. You've been running games since before you could shave, and the galaxy still hasn't caught on.

abilities.name: Born Liar
abilities.rule:
You may use Deception in place of Charm or Persuasion for any social action. You're not charming them — you're deceiving them into feeling charmed. You're not persuading them — you're persuading them with information that isn't true.

Additionally, cross-discipline gambits that modify Charm or Persuasion actions may be applied when you use Deception in their place via Born Liar. The gambit functions identically — it simply triggers off your Deception roll instead.
abilities.shorthand: Use Deception for Charm/Persuasion. Cross-discipline social gambits apply via Deception.

abilities.name: The Hook
abilities.rule:
After resolving a successful Deception action against an NPC, spend 1 tier. You plant a self-propagating lie — a piece of false information so well-constructed that the target not only believes it but acts on it unprompted in future scenes. The lie spreads without your involvement.

The GM determines how the NPC acts on the planted information, but the lie persists until contradicted by direct evidence. The target does not get a second chance to see through it — they already failed.

abilities.name: The Play
abilities.rule:
You construct permanent cover identities for yourself and your crew. This is an uncontested maneuver — roll Deception (Presence). The cover identities include documentation, backstory, and enough planted details to withstand casual inspection. One roll covers the entire crew.

On failure, the covers are created but contain a hidden flaw that you know exists but cannot identify. The GM holds this flaw and may activate it at a dramatically appropriate moment. You know your work wasn't clean — you just don't know where the crack is.
abilities.risk: On failure, covers are built but contain a hidden flaw. You know something is wrong but not what. The GM holds the flaw.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Cover identities hold under casual inspection but step down under active scrutiny. Anyone specifically investigating the crew's identities gets a step-up on their roll.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Solid cover identities. They hold under normal investigation with no step-down. Only dedicated intelligence operations or Force-assisted interrogation might see through them.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Flawless covers. Identities step up under scrutiny — investigation actually reinforces the lie. Digital records, witness accounts, and paper trails all corroborate. Only someone who already knows the truth can see through them.

abilities.name: Deep Cover
abilities.rule:
While operating under cover identities created by The Play (T3), your allies gain a narrative step-up (maximum D8) on any discipline roll where their cover identity would reasonably provide expertise or authority. A cover identity as an Imperial officer grants a step-up on Command rolls when issuing orders to Imperials. A cover as a merchant grants a step-up on Negotiate when haggling.

This is a narrative benefit, not a mechanical override — the step-up cannot exceed D8 regardless of circumstances, and only applies when the cover identity is actively relevant to the action being taken.
abilities.shorthand: Cover identities grant allies narrative step-up (max D8) when identity is relevant.

abilities.name: The Long Con
abilities.rule:
Once per adventure, you may declare that a single event happening right now was something you planned for. You retroactively establish one preparatory action that your character performed off-screen at some point before this moment — and that preparation is now paying off.

The retroactive action must be plausible given your character's capabilities, resources, and the time available. You cannot conjure items from nowhere, kill someone retroactively, or rewrite established events. You can reveal that you bribed the guard last week, planted a weapon under the table yesterday, or arranged for a contact to be in this port today.

The GM has final say on plausibility. If the declaration is reasonable, it simply happens — no roll required. The preparation was already done. You're just revealing it now.

name: The Ichor Witch
description: Ichor flows outward — into wounds, into objects, into the dead. The Ichor Witch channels the magick of Dathomir through physical talismans, sympathetic bonds, and sheer force of will.
fluff: The Nightsisters of Dathomir learned long ago that the Force is not invisible. It has color. It has weight. It has teeth. They were right.

abilities.name: Thread of Ichor
abilities.rule: Your Force expression manifests as visible green ichor energy — unmistakably magick, never subtle. Targets who witness your ichor magick for the first time in a scene gain [Rattled]. You may use Alter (Presence) in place of Medicine (Wits) when performing Treat Injury — you channel ichor into wounds rather than applying conventional medicine.
abilities.shorthand: Use Alter (Presence) for Treat Injury. First-time ichor witness → Rattled.

abilities.name: Ichor Talisman
abilities.rule:
**Creation**: Spend 1 Edge Point during a Narrative scene to imbue a portable object with ichor, creating a Force talisman. One active talisman at a time (until T3). The talisman must be willingly accepted by the bearer — it cannot be planted or hidden on an unwitting target. If not actively carried by the bearer, the talisman is inert. The talisman contains a portion of your Force signature — any Force-sensitive who examines it may identify its creator. In the wrong hands, it can be used to target you.

**Attunement**: When creating the talisman, choose a discipline. The object should reflect the discipline's nature (a weapon for Melee or Ranged, a medpac for Medicine, a lockpick set for Skulduggery, a focus crystal for a Spark discipline, etc. — GM adjudicates). That discipline becomes a favored discipline for the bearer while carried.

**Sense Through the Bond**: While the bearer carries your talisman, you may spend an Action to Assess using Sense (The Spark) through the bearer's senses — no line of sight required. This establishes a sympathetic connection for the remainder of the scene.

**While the connection is active**:
- **Ichor Surge** — Spend 1 of your own Edge Points (no action cost) to trigger an Edge spend for the talisman bearer. This functions as if the bearer spent their own Edge — Tier Boost on their favored disciplines, Unleashed effects, vocation abilities.
- **Pervert the Bond** — Channel the dark side through the connection to use Arise (T4) or True Possession (T5) on or through the bearer — no line of sight required. This immediately flips one Hope to Toll. The talisman blackens and goes inert.

**No connection, no channel.** If you haven't established the Sense link this scene, the talisman is just a charm (the favored discipline still applies passively). Ichor Surge and Pervert the Bond require the active connection.
abilities.shorthand: Spend 1 Edge to imbue an object with ichor. Talisman grants discipline die shift for 1 scene.

abilities.name: Ichor Infusion
abilities.rule:
**Two Talismans**: You may now have 2 active Ichor Talismans simultaneously. Each may be attuned to a different discipline.

**Weapon Infusion**: When the talisman is imbued into a weapon, it retains all T2 talisman properties (attunement, favored discipline, Sense connection, Ichor Surge, Pervert the Bond). Additionally, the weapon gains an ichor-infused quality — attacks with the weapon unlock the Blight gambit.

gambits.name: Blight
gambits.rule: When the bearer hits with an ichor-infused weapon and deals damage, spend 1 tier. The target gains [Rattled]. If the target is already [Rattled], they gain [Exposed] instead.

abilities.shorthand: Two active Talismans. Infuse melee weapon: attacks gain Blight gambit (1 tier → Vitality bleed).

abilities.name: Arise
abilities.rule:
Alter (Presence). Power = Roll. You channel ichor into fallen creatures or corpses in your zone, animating them as thralls. Corpses must be physically present in the fiction.

Directing Thralls: Use the Coordinate maneuver, substituting Control (Grit) for Tactics (Wits). If you are [Incapacitated], all thralls collapse.
abilities.risk: On failure, the ichor dissipates — the dead do not answer. Wasted Action.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Animate 1 corpse as a simple thrall. Acts on your initiative. Collapses at end of round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Animate up to 2 corpses as thralls. Act on your initiative, last until end of scene.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Animate up to 3 corpses as thralls. Act on your initiative, last until end of scene. GM may grant them one echo ability from their living stat block.

abilities.name: True Possession
abilities.rule:
Alter (Presence) vs target's Resolve. You fully project your spirit into the target's body. Your own body goes [Helpless] for the duration. Line of sight required (unless using Pervert the Bond through an Ichor Talisman).

Sustain: 1 Edge Point per round to maintain. If you can't pay, possession ends immediately — you snap back to your body [Disoriented].
abilities.risk: On failure, the target's mind lashes back — you gain [Rattled] + [Disoriented] until end of round. The target knows someone tried to get inside their head.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: The host spirit fights hard. All actions through the possessed body suffer [Disoriented] + [Weakened]. Sustaining also costs your Maneuver each round in addition to 1 Edge.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: The spirit resists but you have the upper hand. Actions through the possessed body suffer [Disoriented] only. Costs 1 Edge per round to sustain.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Total override. Full actions, no penalties. The host spirit is suppressed entirely. Still costs 1 Edge per round. Target retains no memory of what happened during possession.

name: The Juyo Adept
description: Form VII — The Ferocity Form. The forbidden seventh lightsaber discipline, and the last one the Order ever sanctioned. Juyo does not defend. It does not endure. It overwhelms. The practitioner opens themselves to the dark side — not to be consumed by it, but to channel it outward through the blade. Every swing carries the weight of fury held in perfect focus. The superconducting loop: aggression flows in, amplified aggression flows out. The more the enemy fights, the stronger the Adept becomes.
fluff: Only two Jedi ever mastered Vaapad. One of them nearly killed a Sith Lord who had destroyed every other Master sent against him. The other fell to the dark side. The Order forbade this form for good reason. You are learning it anyway.

abilities.name: The Ferocity Form
abilities.rule:
You may roll Melee (Presence) instead of Melee (Physique) when attacking with a lightsaber or light melee weapon. The blade is driven by will, not strength. Every strike carries the weight of your intent — not your body.

When you attack with a lightsaber, light melee weapon, or Alter, you may tap a Toll token from the Destiny Pool. You open yourself to the Force, leaning into the thrill of the fight. For a number of rounds equal to your current Hope count, you gain +1 Effect Tier on all attacks made with a lightsaber, light melee weapon, or Alter.
abilities.shorthand: Melee (Presence) replaces Melee (Physique) with lightsaber or light melee. Tap Toll token: +1 Effect Tier on lightsaber/light melee/Alter attacks for rounds equal to current Hope count.

abilities.name: Vornskyr's Grasp
abilities.rule: Alter (Presence) vs target's Resist. Range 0-1. You reach out through the Force and drag your prey closer.
abilities.risk: On failure, the Force does not answer — the target holds their ground. Wasted Maneuver.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Pull the target up to 1 zone closer to you.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Pull the target up to 1 zone closer. You gain [Empowered] on your next attack this round using Alter or a lightsaber or light melee weapon against that target.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Pull the target up to 1 zone closer. The target presents an Opening — you may Exploit to make an immediate [Empowered] attack using Alter or a lightsaber or light melee weapon.

abilities.shorthand: Alter (Presence) vs Resist, range 0-1. Pull target 1 zone. Masterful: [Empowered] next attack. Legendary: Opening, Exploit for immediate [Empowered] attack.

abilities.name: Ferocity
abilities.rule: When you successfully defend against a Force-related power using Endure or Resist, you gain [Optimized] on your next attack using Alter or a lightsaber or light melee weapon against that target. Their Force aggression feeds your blade.
abilities.shorthand: On successful Endure/Resist vs Force power: gain [Optimized] on next attack against that target.

abilities.name: Redirect
abilities.rule: When you successfully defend against a Force-related power using Endure or Resist, you may spend 1 Edge to redirect the attack back at the caster. Make an Alter (Presence) attack against them. If Ferocity (Tier 3) triggered on the same defense, the redirect is [Optimized].
abilities.shorthand: On successful Endure/Resist vs Force power: spend 1 Edge to redirect as Alter (Presence) attack. Stacks with T3 [Optimized].

abilities.name: The Dark Bargain
abilities.rule: When you deal damage to a target that has attacked you since the end of your last turn, you may flip a Hope token to Toll. Add the new Toll count to the damage dealt. The loop completes — their aggression became yours, and it cost the galaxy a piece of its light.
abilities.shorthand: On damaging a target that attacked you last round: flip Hope to Toll, add new Toll count as bonus damage.

name: The Noble
description: Words are weapons, presence is armor, and the right phrase at the right moment can topple empires. The Noble commands rooms, reads people like open books, and bends wills without ever raising a hand — or a voice.
fluff: Bail Organa held the Senate floor while a star system burned. Mon Mothma whispered three sentences and built a rebellion. Padmé Amidala walked into a war zone in a white dress and made a king kneel. You're not royalty — but when you speak, people listen.

abilities.name: Commanding Presence
abilities.rule: Charm replaces Intimidate for any action where you would normally use Intimidate. You project authority through force of personality rather than threat of violence. When another ability or effect calls for an Intimidate roll, you may use Charm (Presence) instead.
abilities.shorthand: Charm replaces Intimidate. Optimized on first social action per scene.

abilities.name: Read the Room
abilities.rule: Study a target's body language, tone, and social cues. Roll Charm (Presence) — no defense, this is an Assess-style read. You learn what makes them tick and how to use it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn one useful detail about the target's emotional state, motivation, or social leverage.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn the above, plus choose one: gain +1 tier on your next social action against this target, OR your next social action against them is [Optimized].

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Learn the above. You gain both benefits — +1 tier AND [Optimized] on your next social action against the target.

abilities.name: Compel
abilities.rule: Make a demand, issue a directive, or simply make it clear what you expect. Roll Charm (Presence) vs target's Resolve. Pure social pressure — no threats, no lies, just the weight of your personality.
abilities.risk: On failure, the target sees through the play — they are immune to Compel from you for the rest of the scene, and the GM may shift their attitude one step hostile.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target's next interaction with you or your crew is [Disoriented] — they're off-balance, second-guessing.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target must either comply with a reasonable request OR publicly justify their refusal. GM reveals one thing the target is trying to protect or hide.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target complies with your request. Their attitude toward you shifts one step favorable for the rest of the scene.

abilities.name: Entourage
abilities.rule:
When you arrive in a social scene, you may declare one willing NPC present as part of your Entourage. That NPC will carry out reasonable non-combat requests for you for the duration of the scene — run errands, make introductions, hold a position, relay messages.

If you attempt a social action while your Entourage member is visibly supporting you (backing your play, standing at your shoulder), that action gains [Optimized].

Limit: one Entourage member per scene. The GM can rule an NPC unwilling if there's a strong narrative reason.
abilities.shorthand: Declare NPC ally in social scene. Optimized when they visibly support you.

abilities.name: Voice of Authority
abilities.rule:
Upgrades Compel.

**Address the Room:** Your Compel maneuver can now target a group of individuals rather than a single target. Roll once — all targets defend with their own Resolve individually, but the effect tier is determined by your single Control result.

**Sustained Influence:** When you score a Masterful Success (8+ on Control), instead of increasing the effect tier by one level, you may choose to extend the duration of your Compel through the end of the Act. This does not guarantee compliance — but so long as you maintain your charming approach and targets are not endangered, their disposition toward you remains at the level your Compel established. Break the charm (threaten them, put them in danger) and it snaps immediately.
abilities.shorthand: Compel targets groups. Roll once — all targets resist individually.

name: The Telekinetic Savant
description: Raw telekinetic power refined into mastery. The Telekinetic Savant doesn’t just move objects — they weaponize the Force itself. Kinetic Impulse, precision manipulation, and telekinetically wielded weapons are the tools of a practitioner who has turned the most fundamental Force ability into an art form.
fluff: Vader choked admirals from across a Star Destroyer. Dooku hurled Senate platforms like toys. Kreia wielded three lightsabers without touching a single one. Starkiller pulled a Star Destroyer out of orbit. You’re not there yet. But when you reach out with the Force and the room rearranges itself around your will — that’s the path you’re walking.

abilities.name: Kinetic Impulse
abilities.rule: Project the Force outward to push or pull a target through space. Roll Alter (Presence) vs target’s Defense. Choose push or pull direction on hit. This is weaponized telekinesis — directing the Force at people, not just objects.
abilities.risk: Losing control of the Force may cause unintended collateral damage.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Push or pull the target 1 zone in any direction.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Push or pull the target 2 zones. Target gains [Disoriented] Tactical.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Push or pull the target 3 zones. Target gains [Prone]. If the target collides with a solid boundary before completing their forced movement, they take 1 Vitality damage per zone of movement not completed.

abilities.name: Precision Telekinesis
abilities.rule: You gain narrative permission to perform fine, complex manipulations with the Telekinesis maneuver — tasks that would normally require hands, tools, or direct physical contact. Examples: extracting a slug from a wound, typing an access code from across the room, picking a lock mechanism, disarming a detonator, threading a cable, removing a restraint bolt. The GM still sets difficulty based on complexity, but the permission is granted — your telekinesis is a scalpel when you need it to be, not just a battering ram. This does NOT grant discipline substitution — if the task calls for Medicine or Tech, you still roll that discipline. You just don’t need to be touching the thing.
abilities.shorthand: Fine telekinetic manipulation: pick locks, press buttons, write at distance.

abilities.name: Kinetic Combat
abilities.rule: Telekinetically wield a weapon — it fights independently, guided by your will. Roll Alter (Presence) to activate. The weapon can target enemies in your zone or an adjacent zone. Attacks made through Kinetic Combat use Melee (Presence) — attribute swap. Sustained: spend your Maneuver each round to maintain (no additional roll). To re-roll for a better tier, you may roll again instead of sustaining — failure means the weapon drops wherever it was. If you do not spend your Maneuver to sustain, the weapon falls.
abilities.risk: Failure on activation or re-establishment means the weapon drops — you lose telekinetic control.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: The weapon can Attack as your Action using Melee (Presence). Raw strikes only — no Gambits, no Exploits. Your control is sufficient to fight, but not to finesse.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting, but your form is precise enough to use any qualifying Gambits on the attack. GM discretion on edge cases requiring physical contact.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful, plus the weapon can use Exploits. Full combat capability through pure telekinetic mastery.

abilities.name: Force Grip
abilities.rule: After rolling Kinetic Impulse, spend 1 tier. Remove all forced movement from the result. Instead, deal Vitality damage equal to the number of zones the reduced tier would have generated (Fleeting: 1, Masterful: 2, Legendary: 3). Target gains [Restrained] Tactical. The effect lasts 1 round. To maintain: spend your Action next turn and roll Kinetic Impulse again. Success continues the grip and reapplies damage. If you do not roll, the target is freed.

abilities.name: Telekinetic Mastery
abilities.rule:
You have achieved total command of telekinetic Force projection. Three benefits:

**Scale Mastery:** You ignore the first step-down penalty when using any telekinetic Force ability (Telekinesis, Kinetic Impulse, Force Grip, Kinetic Combat) against targets of larger scale. Speeder-scale targets impose no penalty. Starship-scale targets impose only 1 step-down instead of 2.

gambits.name: Split Focus
gambits.rule: When using any targeted TK ability (Telekinesis, Kinetic Impulse, Kinetic Combat, Force Grip), you may apply the effect to two separate targets. Both targets must be in the same targetable area (same zone or adjacent zone as the ability allows).

gambits.name: Force Repulse
gambits.rule: Modifies Kinetic Impulse. Range 0 (your zone only). Instead of targeting a single enemy, the impulse affects ALL targets in your zone — allies included. Everyone gets pushed. The Kinetic Impulse tier effect applies to all targets equally.

abilities.shorthand: Ignore first scale step-down. Hurl objects as attacks. Sustain grip without action cost.

name: The Ace
description: Throttle forward, stick back, and trust the ship. The Ace is a born pilot who reads starship combat the way ground fighters read a room — angles, closure rates, and the split-second window between a clean shot and a hull breach. Whether it's a freighter through an asteroid field or a starfighter through a capital ship's screen, the Ace puts the ship where it needs to be.
fluff: Wedge Antilles survived two Death Star runs. Hera Syndulla flew a VCX-100 through an Imperial blockade sideways. Han Solo threaded the Kessel Run through a maelstrom that ate Star Destroyers for breakfast. They all had the same thing — not luck, not the Force, just an instinct for where the ship wants to go and the nerve to let it. You've got that instinct. Now you need a ship worth flying.
designNotes: The Ace operates on the vocation layer — above the base station actions (anyone in the seat) and separate from hardware modifications (the ship's capability). The Ace makes the pilot better regardless of the ship. Tier 1 changes HOW the pilot shoots (discipline swap). Tier 2 adds a new setup maneuver the station doesn't have (Strafing Run). Tier 3 enhances Dogfight economy (spend tier to gain extra actions). Tier 4 turns Evade into an offensive weapon (exploit the enemy's aggression). Tier 5 breaks the rules entirely (attempt the impossible). None of these duplicate hardware mods — they stack on top of whatever ship the Ace is flying.

abilities.name: Seat of the Pants
abilities.rule: When firing pilot-controlled ship weapons (forward cannons, linked guns, turret you're manning), you may use Piloting (Reflex) in place of Heavy Weapons (Physique) for the attack roll — you aim with the ship, not the gun. This is critical for Dogfight Openings at Masterful+: any pilot can take the reactive shot, but the Ace takes it with their best stat. Additionally, when you score 8+ on your Join Battle roll while piloting, you may immediately take one Piloting maneuver (Fly) before the first round begins — you read the battlefield before anyone else.
abilities.shorthand: Use Piloting (Reflex) for ship weapons. Join Battle 8+ → free Fly maneuver.

abilities.name: Strafing Run
abilities.rule: Piloting (Reflex) vs target's Resolve. A precision setup — the Ace pushes the ship into an aggressive approach vector: diving, strafing, or lining up a deflection shot that forces the target to react. This is the Ace's personal attack setup, distinct from the station's Attack Run (which buffs Gunners). The Strafing Run sets up the Ace's own next shot. Usable both inside and outside a Dogfight — the Ace can set up their own kill shot during a turning fight.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — your approach was too predictable and they have the angle.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Your next ship weapon attack against this target before the end of your next turn gains [Optimized].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Your next ship weapon attack against this target gains [Optimized] and [Empowered].

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled] and [Exposed]. Your next ship weapon attack against this target gains [Optimized] and [Empowered].

abilities.name: Punch It
abilities.rule: When you win a Dogfight and choose to disengage, spend 1 tier. You break free with explosive force — move up to 2 zones instead of 1, and all ships that had [Locked On] on you lose contact entirely (they cannot close or engage until the start of the next round). This is the Han Solo escape: punch the throttle, leave the furball behind, and nobody can follow. Usable in any Dogfight disengage — ground vehicle chases, atmospheric pursuits, or space combat.

abilities.name: Thread the Needle
abilities.rule: When you Evade and score 8+ on your Control die, you may forgo the standard +1 Effect tier bonus and the Opening it generates. Instead, choose one: (a) you fly through terrain that the attacker cannot follow — breaking line of sight and gaining concealment until your next turn, disrupting Dogfight engagement (the attacker must re-enter the Dogfight next round), or (b) you redirect the incoming attack into an environmental hazard or another hostile ship in your zone — the GM determines the collision or friendly-fire damage. The Ace turns defense into a weapon.

abilities.name: Impossible Maneuver
abilities.rule: Once per Act, when the GM declares a piloting challenge impossible — too narrow, too fast, too many guns, too many Dogfight opponents — you may attempt it anyway. Roll Piloting (Reflex) against the GM's stated difficulty. On any success, you pull it off: the GM narrates the impossible becoming possible. On a Masterful+ result, you also gain [Optimized] on all Piloting actions for the remainder of the scene — you and the crew are riding the high of having just done the undoable. On failure, the ship takes critical damage but you survive — the Ace always walks away from the crash. This ability explicitly overrides Dogfight engagement constraints: the Ace can attempt to disengage from multiple Dogfights simultaneously, thread impossible terrain at full speed, or execute maneuvers that should not be physically possible for the ship's class.

name: The Blade Dancer
description: Speed over strength, precision through motion. The Blade Dancer wields light weapons with preternatural agility — twin blades tracing lethal arcs faster than the eye can follow. Where brutes swing heavy iron, you have already cut three times and moved on.
fluff: Ahsoka Tano reversed her grips and carved through MagnaGuards like they were standing still. Asajj Ventress danced through clone troopers with paired curved-hilts, each kill flowing into the next. A Mandalorian knife-fighter on Concordia who never drew the same blade twice. A Twi'lek assassin on Nar Shaddaa who could open four throats before the first body hit the floor. The Blade Dancer doesn't overpower. They overwhelm.

abilities.name: Light Step
abilities.rule: When wielding a Light melee weapon or Lightsaber, you may attack using Melee (Reflex) instead of Melee (Physique). You may draw or ready Light melee weapons as part of any Attack action at no Maneuver cost.
abilities.shorthand: Attack with Melee (Reflex) using Light melee/Lightsaber. No Dodge Openings while wielding.

abilities.name: Twin Blades
abilities.rule: When dual-wielding Light melee weapons or Lightsabers, you ignore the Control die step-down penalty. You keep the Power die step-up. Gambits from either equipped weapon may be declared on any Light melee or Lightsaber attack.
abilities.shorthand: Dual-wield Light melee/Lightsaber: no Control step-down. Cross-weapon gambits.

abilities.name: Relentless
abilities.rule: After resolving a Light melee or Lightsaber attack that deals damage, spend 1 Effect tier. The target immediately presents an Opening. If you are dual-wielding, you may Exploit this Opening with your off-hand weapon as a reaction.

abilities.name: Slip Strike
abilities.rule: When you Dodge a melee attack and score 8+ on your Control die, the attacker immediately presents an Opening that you may Exploit with a Melee attack. Additionally, at this tier you may spend Edge to fuel Melee Exploits.

abilities.name: Tempest
abilities.rule: Assess using Tactics (Wits). Study all targets in your zone or an adjacent zone. The number of Assess questions scored determines how many targets you may designate (up to 3). Your next attack action this turn targets all designated targets provided they are in the same zone. Make a single attack roll — use the highest Presence among the designated targets for your Control die, then apply your Power die to each target individually (each target's defense rating may produce different results). You end your action Engaged in that zone with any survivors. On subsequent rounds, the assessment holds — spend the Maneuver (no roll required) and 1 Edge to activate the multi-target attack again.

name: The Ghost
description: Misdirection, concealment, and the art of not being where anyone expects you. The Ghost doesn't fight fair — they fight from positions the enemy didn't know existed, and when the walls close in, they get everyone out.
fluff: Intelligence operatives, resistance saboteurs, and the kind of people who walk through Imperial checkpoints without anyone remembering their face. The Ghost doesn't win fights. They win extractions. They win infiltrations. They win by making the enemy argue about whether anyone was ever really there. Cassian Andor walked into Scarif knowing he wasn't coming back. You walk into places knowing you will.

abilities.name: Gray Man
abilities.rule: When you benefit from concealment, you may use Stealth (Reflex) in place of Evasion on Dodge defense rolls. Additionally, while benefiting from concealment, you do not present Openings originating from the Engaged condition.
abilities.shorthand: Use Stealth (Reflex) for Dodge while concealed. No Engaged Openings while concealed.

abilities.name: Blind Spot
abilities.rule: When you Dodge an attack from an Engaged target, spend 1 tier. Your next attack against that target before the end of your next turn gains [Optimized].

abilities.name: Slip
abilities.rule: Stealth (Reflex) vs target's Resolve. Use movement and misdirection to fade from a target's focus — a feint, a peripheral slide, blending into the chaos. Risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you tried to be clever and they saw right through it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: You gain concealment from the target. Duration: Immediate.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: You gain concealment from the target. Duration: Tactical (until start of your next turn).

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: You gain concealment from the target. Duration: Tactical. Immediately move 1 zone.

abilities.name: Vanishing Act
abilities.rule: When you Dodge and your Control die is 8+, spend your Exploit pip. You gain concealment from the attacker. Duration: Tactical.

abilities.name: Exit Strategy
abilities.rule: Once per Act, if all allies and yourself have concealment and none are restrained or held in place, you may declare an Opening for all allies and yourself explicitly to escape the scene. There must be a plausible means of egress, even if unconventional — a grate, a shaft, a window, a crowd. The GM narrates the escape.

name: The Gunslinger
description: Fast hands, sharp instincts, and a blaster that never leaves your side. The Gunslinger is a pistol specialist who thrives in the chaos of close-to-mid range firefights — drawing first, shooting last, and making every Opening count.
fluff: They say Cad Bane could clear a room before anyone heard the first shot. Captain Rex would draw both DC-17s mid-roll and put rounds on target before he hit the ground. Han Solo shot Greedo under a table because he was bored of the conversation. You're not that good. Not yet.

abilities.name: Born to the Blaster
abilities.rule: Firing a Pistol while Engaged does not present an Opening. You may draw or holster Pistols as part of any Attack action at no Maneuver cost. On a successful Join Battle, you may immediately draw one or both Pistols.
abilities.shorthand: No Opening firing Engaged. Draw/holster Pistols free on Attack. Draw on Join Battle.

abilities.name: Akimbo
abilities.rule: When dual wielding Pistols, you ignore the Control die step-down penalty. You keep the Power die step-up. Gambits from either equipped Pistol may be declared on any Pistol attack.
abilities.shorthand: Dual-wield Pistols: no Control step-down. Cross-pistol gambits allowed.

abilities.name: Dead Drop
abilities.rule: Ranged (Reflex) vs target's Resolve. Draw down on a target and force them to react — you don't fire, you just let them see where this is going. Risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you've shown your hand.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Choose one: the target must spend their next Maneuver taking Cover, OR they present an Opening to you.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled] and [Exposed]. They present an Opening to you. If you Exploit it this round, step up Effect by 1 Tier.

abilities.name: Fan the Hammer
abilities.rule: After resolving a Pistol attack, spend 1 tier. If your Control die result was 8+ and the target takes damage, the target immediately presents an Opening to you which you may Exploit.

abilities.name: Two for Flinching
abilities.rule: When you Exploit an Opening with a Pistol and the attack deals damage, the target presents another Opening to any ally in the zone. Your shots crack the door wide open for the whole crew.
abilities.shorthand: Exploit Opening + Pistol damage → target presents Opening to ally in zone.

name: The Doc
description: The Doc keeps the crew alive. Not through miracles or Force healing — through skill, preparation, and the steady hands that come from knowing exactly what you're doing when everyone else is panicking. They make medical resources last longer, treat injuries more effectively, and when someone goes down at the end of a fight, they're the one you want standing over the body.
fluff: Every crew in the Outer Rim knows the same truth: you can replace a pilot, you can find another gunner, but a good medic is worth their weight in aurodium. The Doc isn't flashy. They don't perform miracles. They just make sure that when the smoke clears and someone isn't moving, they start moving again. That's the job. That's all of it.

abilities.name: Field Medic
abilities.rule:
When you are adjacent to an ally and not Engaged, you may apply medical consumables (Bacta Patches, Stim Packs, Bacta Geltabs, and similar combat medical items) as a Maneuver instead of an Action. You still require the item and must meet all other requirements for its use.

Additionally, when you Assess a patient using Medicine (Wits), you may convert one Assess question into [Optimized] on your next Medicine action targeting that patient. Your diagnosis informs your treatment — you identified the condition and you know exactly how to address it.
abilities.shorthand: Apply medical items as Maneuver when adjacent. Assess patient → Optimized next Medicine.

abilities.name: Precise Dosage
abilities.rule:
When you perform Treat Injury, ignore 1 level of [Stimmed] when determining your Medicine Power die step-down. A patient at [Stimmed 3] is treated as [Stimmed 2] by you.

The [Stimmed] condition itself remains on the patient at its actual value — if another healer treats them, they face the full penalty. You are not removing the drugs from their system. You are skilled enough to work through the chemical interference.
abilities.shorthand: Ignore 1 Stimmed level on Treat Injury.

abilities.name: Specialist
abilities.rule:
Your Treat Injury results are enhanced at every tier:

**Fleeting:** Increase Trauma recovery by 2 instead of 1 for all allies resting with you. (Short rest: 3 Trauma instead of 1. Long rest: 4 instead of 2.)

**Masterful:** As Fleeting, plus allies may convert 3 Vitality into 1 additional Trauma recovery instead of 2.

**Legendary:** Increase Vitality regeneration by 2 instead of 1 for all allies. (Short rest: recover 2 Vitality. Long rest: recover 3 Vitality.)
abilities.shorthand: Enhanced Treat Injury at every tier. Bonus Trauma/Vitality recovery.

abilities.name: Adrenaline Protocol
abilities.rule:
Expend a Stim Pack or Medpac on an adjacent ally (as a Maneuver via Field Medic). The target gains [Stimmed] +1 as normal. Choose one:

**Overdrive:** For a number of rounds equal to the target's current [Stimmed] level (after this application), their Reflex and Physique are stepped up. At the end of the target's turn when the duration expires, they crash — Reflex and Physique are stepped down until a Short Rest.

**Sedate:** The target is rendered unconscious. On enemies, requires a successful Medicine (Wits) action to administer.

Additionally, your Precise Dosage (T2) now ignores 2 levels of [Stimmed] instead of 1.

abilities.name: Resuscitation Protocol
abilities.rule: When you perform a Stabilize (Interact) on an incapacitated ally, your result is increased by +1 effect tier. Your Fleeting result achieves what others need a Masterful for. Your Masterful achieves what others need a Legendary for. And your Legendary result exceeds what anyone else in the galaxy can accomplish with the same tools and the same patient.
abilities.shorthand: +1 effect tier on Stabilize. Fleeting stops bleed. Masterful restores 1 Vitality.

name: The Hound
description: You don't chase your prey — you make their world smaller. Every contact they call is one you've already spoken to. Every safe house they run to is one you've already mapped. By the time they see your face, the hunt is already over. They just don't know it yet.
fluff: The Bounty Hunters' Guild doesn't train Hounds — it recognizes them. They're the ones who return the impossible bounties, the ones who find the target everyone else gave up on three systems ago. Ask a Hound how they do it and they'll tell you there's no trick to it: you study the mark, you work the contacts, you follow the thread, and you don't stop. Ever. Some hunters are fast. Some are deadly. A Hound is inevitable.

abilities.name: Acquire the Scent
abilities.rule:
Once per episode, if you do not already have a Mark, you may declare one. Your Mark is a specific individual — a bounty contract, a personal target, or someone the hunt demands you find. This Mark persists until they are acquired, killed, or the GM agrees a change is warranted. You may only have one active Mark at a time.

While in direct pursuit of your Mark, you may substitute Wits for Presence as the governing Arena when making Assess or Interact maneuvers during narrative scenes. This applies only to actions directly related to the hunt — questioning informants about your Mark's whereabouts, intimidating a contact into giving up a safe house, reading a room for your Mark's associates. It won't help you charm your way into unrelated social situations.
abilities.shorthand: Declare a Mark (1/episode). Wits replaces Presence on hunt-related Assess/Interact in narrative scenes.

abilities.name: Closing In
abilities.rule:
While pursuing your Mark, each successful Assess or Interact maneuver directly related to the hunt generates a Lead — a piece of actionable intelligence. You may hold up to 3 Leads at a time. Leads expire when you change your Mark.

When the trail has gone cold — when you've hit a dead end, failed a critical roll, or simply can't see the next step — you may spend one Lead to go back over the information you've gathered. The GM must provide one actionable, truthful piece of intel or direction that moves the hunt forward. This isn't new information appearing from nowhere — it's something you already had that suddenly clicks into place.
abilities.shorthand: Successful hunt Assess/Interact → generate a Lead (max 3). Spend 1 Lead when stuck → GM provides actionable intel.

abilities.name: Secondary Acquisition
abilities.rule:
You may designate a Secondary Mark in addition to your primary Mark. The Secondary Mark may be declared once per Act and does not require GM approval to change. It does not replace or affect your primary Mark in any way.

Your T1 and T2 abilities (Wits-for-Presence swap on hunt-related Assess/Interact, and Lead generation) apply to your Secondary Mark as well. However, Leads generated from the Secondary Mark draw from the same pool (still max 3).

Experienced Hounds use their Secondary Mark to run down associates of their primary target, take contracts of opportunity, or lend their talents to the crew's current objective — all without losing the scent on their real quarry.
abilities.shorthand: Designate a Secondary Mark (1/Act, no GM approval needed). T1/T2 abilities apply to both Marks. Shared Lead pool.

abilities.name: The Right Questions
abilities.rule:
When you make an Assess or Interact maneuver in direct pursuit of a Mark, you may invoke this Gambit: accept a -1 Tier shift on the effect of the roll, and generate +1 Lead in addition to the roll's normal outcome (even on a Fleeting success). You know the right questions to ask — sometimes the answer matters more than the impression.

Additionally, you may now spend a Lead as you would spend Edge when directly confronting your Mark, regardless of scene type. The preparation pays off — every Lead represents something you learned during the hunt that gives you an edge in the moment of truth.
abilities.shorthand: Gambit: -1 Tier on Assess/Interact → +1 Lead. Leads may be spent as Edge when confronting your Mark.

abilities.name: Dead to Rights
abilities.rule: Upon the initial confrontation with your Mark — the first time they see you in a scene — you may make an immediate Tactics (Wits) attack vs the target's Resolve. You've tailored your approach, chosen the moment, and made sure they know exactly who's standing in front of them.
abilities.risk: On failure, your Mark is unimpressed — they've been hunted before. They may act first in the resulting encounter.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Your Mark gains [Rattled]. They know what you are. The hunt is over and they feel it.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Your Mark gains [Stunned]. The weight of realization hits them — you found them, and there's nowhere left to go.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Your Mark gains [Stunned]. Choose one: Demand Surrender — you must possess enough Leads to match or exceed the target's Presence score (1-for-1). If met, the Mark surrenders unless they possess a trait that allows them to resist. Leads are not spent if the Mark does not surrender. Strike First — the Mark immediately presents an Opening to you. You may Exploit it as a reaction, then take the first full combat turn (Maneuver and Action) without a Join Battle roll. Combat proceeds normally afterward. In either case, if the Mark refuses surrender, they immediately present an Opening to you. You may Exploit it as a reaction and take the first full combat turn before anyone else acts. Unspent Leads remain available as Edge for the confrontation.

name: The Investigator
description: Notices what others miss, connects what others can't, and arrives at the truth before anyone else knows there's a question. The Investigator doesn't fight harder or talk smoother — they simply know more, see more, and understand the significance of what they find.
fluff: Obi-Wan traced a single saberdart through a deleted archive to uncover a secret clone army. Cassian Andor survived fifteen years of intelligence work by reading rooms faster than anyone in them. Thrawn studied a species' art and reverse-engineered their entire battle doctrine. You've been doing this long enough that the galaxy stopped surprising you — now it just confirms what you already suspected.

abilities.name: Trained Eye
abilities.rule:
You may use Investigation in place of Insight when attempting to read an NPC target — you read people through observation and evidence rather than empathy. You notice the inconsistency in their story, not the tremor in their voice.

Additionally, even on a failed Assess, you still learn one detail related to the assessment target. You never walk away completely empty-handed.
abilities.shorthand: Use Investigation for Insight to read NPCs. Passive observation, no social engagement.

abilities.name: Connect the Dots
abilities.rule:
When you succeed on an Assess, you may spend one of your earned questions to instead make a **declaration**: state a connection between two facts, people, or events you've encountered this session. The GM must confirm if you're correct, partially correct, or wrong — and if partially correct, they adjust your declaration to the truth rather than simply saying no.

This isn't asking the GM for information — it's proposing a theory and forcing the narrative to respond.
abilities.shorthand: Spend Assess question to declare a connection. GM confirms or denies.

abilities.name: Keen Insight
abilities.rule:
Dedicate your full attention to a single person, object, or location that the GM confirms is narratively significant — a crime scene, a key NPC, a suspicious artifact, a location tied to an active lead. If the target holds no relevant information, the GM may simply say "Nothing to see here" and the maneuver is not spent.

This goes deeper than Assess — you're not asking questions, you're reconstructing what happened.
abilities.risk: Tunnel vision. You are fully absorbed — you cannot act on or respond to anything else happening in the scene until your next beat. If the situation shifts around you, you miss it entirely.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn the most recent significant event involving the subject — who was here, what they did, approximately when.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn the above, plus the GM reveals a hidden detail — something concealed, altered, or deliberately obscured. A false trail, a removed object, a covered wound.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Full reconstruction. The GM narrates a brief flashback of what happened at this location or to this subject. You see it play out. The crew may act on this as reliable intelligence.

abilities.name: Follow the Thread
abilities.rule:
Upgrades Keen Insight.

When you score a Masterful Success (8+ on Control) on Keen Insight, after resolving the reconstruction, you may immediately make an Assess maneuver at no additional action cost. This follow-up Assess gains +1 Tier to its effect result.

Your questions on this Assess must pertain directly to details revealed by the Keen Insight reconstruction — you're drilling into what you just saw, not fishing for new information.
abilities.shorthand: Keen Insight Masterful 8+ → spend Assess question for a second reconstruction angle.

abilities.name: The Confrontation
abilities.rule:
When you confront the target of your investigation — the person, group, or entity your Keen Insight and Assess results have been building toward — you gain [Optimized] on all Assess and social actions directed at them. You know their patterns, their tells, their pressure points. You've done the work.

If the confrontation turns hostile, you may spend 1 Edge Point. All allies gain [Optimized] and [Empowered] on their Join Battle rolls. You saw this coming. You briefed the crew. Nobody's caught flat-footed.
abilities.shorthand: Confront investigation target: Optimized and Empowered on first action against them. 1/arc.

name: The Rifleman
description: The Rifleman is not an assassin, not a gunfighter, and not a hero. He is a professional — a light infantryman trained to read terrain, hold positions, and make the squad more lethal than its parts. He fights from cover, moves by bounds, and when the line is threatened, he calls the fire that stops everything cold. The rifle is his tool. His weapon is his mind.
fluff: The old Alderaanian Regulars had a saying: 'The rifleman is the army.' Not the walkers, not the starfleet, not the officer corps. The rifleman. The professional who reads the ground, holds the line, and puts rounds on target while the galaxy burns around him. Every army in history has learned this lesson. Most forget it between wars.

abilities.name: Calculated Shooter
abilities.rule:
When you Aim with a Rifle, use Wits as your Power die instead of Reflex. You do not snap-shoot — you calculate angles, read wind, and track movement before you pull the trigger.

While in Cover, you never need to spend a Maneuver pip to maintain it. Holding position, watching lines of fire, and tracking enemy vectors is second nature. You still must use Take Cover to establish your position — this ability only waives the round-to-round maintenance cost.
abilities.shorthand: Wits replaces Reflex as Aim Power die with Rifles. Free Cover maintenance — no pip cost to hold position.

abilities.name: Battlefield Read
abilities.rule:
After resolving an Aim, spend 1 tier. Ask the GM one tactical question about the combat zone — not about the target you are aiming at, but about everything else. Your trained eye absorbs the full scene while your scope is trained.

Examples: Where is the best cover in the adjacent zone? Are there overwatch positions above us? What hazards are in the eastern corridor? Where are the enemy reinforcement routes? What is the quality of cover the target is using?

The answer is honest and based on what a trained eye could reasonably observe from your current position and line of sight. This is not an Assess on the target — it is the sum of everything your senses take in while your rifle is up.
abilities.shorthand: After Aim, spend 1 tier. Ask GM one tactical question about the combat zone (not the target).

abilities.name: Leapfrog
abilities.rule: You and one ally reposition together — bounding movement, covering each other as you advance or withdraw. You spend your Maneuver pip to initiate. The ally spends their Exploit pip to move.
abilities.risk: The ally must spend their Exploit pip to participate. If the Rifleman fails the roll, both characters remain in place and the pips are spent.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: You and the ally each move 1 zone. If cover is available in the destination zone, each of you may Take Cover as a free maneuver (still rolls Tactics for Cover value).

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting, plus either you or the ally moves 1 additional zone.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful, plus the ally may make a free Ranged attack exploit against a target the Rifleman has fired at this round.

abilities.shorthand: Tactics (Wits). Both move 1 zone + free Take Cover. Masterful: +1 zone. Legendary: ally gets free Ranged exploit.

abilities.name: Affix Bayonets
abilities.rule:
While you have a Rifle equipped, you may use it as a Melee weapon using Melee (Physique). Defaults to Light chassis — butt stroke, stock slam — unless a Bayonet or other melee attachment alters the chassis.

When an enemy enters your Engaged zone while you are in Cover, they present an Opening which you may Exploit with your Melee attack. The blade meets them before they can close — they thought rushing a dug-in rifleman was the smart play. It was not.
abilities.shorthand: Rifle usable as Melee weapon (Physique, Light chassis unless attachment alters). Enemies entering Engaged zone while in Cover present an Opening for Melee exploit.

abilities.name: Final Protective Line
abilities.rule:
When you activate Coordinated Fire (Tactics D8 gambit on Aim), you may spend 1 Edge. If you do, the Aim benefit is shared with all allies who can hear you, not just one. All eligible targets in the aimed target's zone present an Opening to those allies. Allies may spend their Exploit pip to make a Ranged attack reaction against any target in that zone. All attacks made this way gain [Optimized].

Fire on my mark. You call the line, you take aim, and the squad lights up the zone.
abilities.shorthand: On Coordinated Fire, spend 1 Edge: Aim benefit to ALL allies. Targets in zone present Openings. Allies Exploit with [Optimized].

name: The Seer
description: The Force is a river of information — past, present, and the faintest currents of what may come. The Seer drinks from that river. They perceive what others cannot: the fracture lines in people and events, the split-second warnings that turn impossible dodges into casual sidesteps, and the shatterpoints where everything can break.
fluff: Quinlan Vos touched a wall and watched a murder happen three days after the blood dried. Cal Kestis picked up a fallen lightsaber and lived its owner’s last stand. Mace Windu saw the fracture in the Confederacy before anyone else knew it existed. You’re not that powerful. Not yet. But when the Force shows you the crack in everything — pay attention.

abilities.name: Combat Sense
