abilities.rule: You may use Sense (Wits) in place of Tactics (Wits) for Join Battle rolls. The Force whispers the shape of violence before it begins. Additionally, on a Masterful Success (Control 8+) on Join Battle, choose one: gain +1 Effect Tier on your initiative result, OR gain [Optimized] on all Attack and Defense rolls until the end of your first Action.
abilities.shorthand: Use Sense (Wits) for Join Battle. On success, sense one hidden or concealed combatant.

abilities.name: Force Sight
abilities.rule: You may use Sense (Wits) in place of any discipline normally called for on Assess actions. Your perception extends beyond physical senses — you ignore darkness, cover, concealment, and solid barriers when assessing. You detect life presences, emotional states, and Force signatures. This does not replace Sense discipline gambits — those still function normally on top of this substitution. The GM adjudicates effective range based on the fiction.
abilities.shorthand: Use Sense (Wits) for any Assess. Perceive lies, intent, danger through the Force.

abilities.name: Shatterpoint Sense
abilities.rule: Perceive the connections between people, events, and objects through the Force. Roll Sense (Wits), no defense. You do not learn facts — you perceive relationships, emotional weight, and the web of causality that binds things together.
abilities.risk: On failure, you gain [Disoriented]. Particularly traumatic echoes may add [Rattled] at the GM’s discretion.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: A vague sense that a connection exists between two elements in the scene. You know something links them, but not the nature or significance.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: You perceive the nature and emotional weight of the connection. The GM reveals the relationship’s character — loyalty, fear, debt, hatred, love, obligation. You may ask 1 question about the connection.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: The full web of connections unfolds. You see how multiple elements relate — the chain of causality, the pressure points, the fault lines. The GM reveals the key pressure point in the web. You may ask 2 questions.

abilities.name: Precognitive Counter
abilities.rule: When you Endure or Dodge and your Control die is 8+, spend your Exploit pip. Instead of the standard +1 Tier bonus from Masterful Success, the attacker presents an Opening to you. You saw the strike before it landed and positioned yourself to punish it.

abilities.name: Shatterpoint Strike
abilities.rule: Once per scene. Roll Sense (Wits) vs target’s Resolve. You perceive the shatterpoint — the exact fracture line in the target’s defense, stance, or resolve. You must attack the target before the end of the round or the moment passes. If the attack is not made, the shatterpoint closes and the maneuver is wasted.
abilities.risk: On failure, the maneuver is wasted and the target knows you tried to read them — they sense the probe.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed] and [Optimized] applies to your next attack against them this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed]. Your next attack against them this round gains [Optimized] and [Empowered].

name: The Ship's Mechanic
description: A master of starship systems, vehicle repair, and mid-combat optimization who keeps the vessel flying and buffs the crew's vehicle actions.
fluff: Every ship has a heartbeat — the low hum of a reactor cycling, the whine of a converter pulling too hard, the silence right before something breaks. You hear all of it. While the Ace is pulling maneuvers and the gunner is lighting up the void, you're three decks down with your hands inside a junction box, rerouting power through conduits that weren't designed for it, coaxing one more jump out of a hyperdrive that should have been scrapped two parsecs ago. You're the reason the ship comes home.

abilities.name: Chief Engineer
abilities.rule: At the start of each adventure, designate one starship or vehicle under your crew's control. For the duration of the adventure, you are assumed to always have the right tools and minor repair components on hand when working on this vessel. Additionally, while seated or stationed as this vessel's engineer, you cannot be afflicted by the [Disoriented] condition.
abilities.shorthand: Designate ship per adventure. Auto-equipped for repairs. Immune to [Disoriented] at Engineer station.

abilities.name: Emergency Shunt
abilities.rule: While you are stationed on your designated vehicle and it suffers damage that reduces its Integrity, you may spend 1 of your personal Edge as a reaction to prevent 1 point of that damage.
abilities.shorthand: Spend 1 Edge as reaction to prevent 1 ship Integrity damage.

abilities.name: Optimal Calibration
abilities.rule: When you make a successful Assess roll regarding a starship or vehicle's status, you may spend 1 Tier to convert your earned questions into Calibration Charges (1 charge per remaining tier). Until the start of your next turn, whenever an ally rolls one of the vessel's systems as a Power Die, you may spend 1 Calibration Charge to allow them to reroll that Power Die prior to finalizing the result. They keep the higher result.

abilities.name: Advanced Calibration
abilities.rule: Your Optimal Calibration ability expands. You may now choose to let an ally spend a Calibration Charge to reroll their Control Die instead of their Power Die. They still may not reroll both on the same action.
abilities.shorthand: Calibration Charges now also allow Control Die reroll (one or the other).

abilities.name: Overcharged Routing
abilities.rule: When you successfully perform the Reroute Power maneuver, you may spend 1 Tier from the result. If you do, the system whose die was increased gains the Unleashed capability (the die explodes on a maximum result) for as long as it remains affected by the maneuver.

abilities.name: Miracle Worker
abilities.rule: Once per adventure, when an ally on your designated vessel makes a roll using a die (Control or Power) that you have modified, buffed, or allowed them to reroll since the start of your last turn, you may spend 1 Edge to instantly set that die to its maximum natural result instead of rolling it. If applied to a Power die with the Unleashed capability, it automatically triggers the explosion.

name: The Soresu Guardian
description: Form III — The Way of the Mynock. The Soresu Guardian is the Jedi who chose to stop chasing glory and start keeping people alive. Form III was born in the age of blasters — when lightsabers met a galaxy that fought at range, and the old dueling forms couldn't answer the volume of fire. Soresu answered it. Not with speed, not with aggression, but with patience. Economy. The smallest movement that turns death aside.
fluff: Be a wall. Every bolt finds the blade. Every second they spend firing is a second they are losing. A Soresu Guardian does not win fights. The enemy loses them.

abilities.name: Shien Gate
abilities.rule: When you Endure a Ranged attack while wielding a lightsaber, you may use Sense Spark in place of Endure as your defense discipline. You roll Sense Spark (Physique). The Force whispers the bolt's path before the trigger is pulled. The blade is already there.
abilities.shorthand: Sense Spark replaces Endure discipline vs Ranged attacks with lightsaber. Roll Sense Spark (Physique).

abilities.name: Reflect
abilities.rule: When you Endure a Ranged attack while wielding a lightsaber, if your Control die rolls 8+: instead of gaining the standard +1 tier, you may spend your Exploit pip to redirect the bolt at any target within 5 zones. This redirected attack is rolled using Sense (Wits). You don't aim — you already know where they are.
abilities.shorthand: On Endure vs Ranged with lightsaber, Control 8+: trade +1 tier, spend Exploit pip to redirect bolt at target within 5 zones. Attack is Sense (Wits).

abilities.name: Resilience Stance
abilities.rule: You plant your feet and let the Form take over. Tight arcs. Minimal movement. The blade becomes a wall of light. Costs a Maneuver pip each round to maintain. If you leave your zone, the stance breaks immediately.
abilities.risk: If the roll fails, you do not enter the stance. The Maneuver pip is spent.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Gain [Optimized] on all defense rolls against Ranged attacks.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Gain [Optimized] on all defense rolls against Ranged attacks. You act as Cover 2 in your zone — allies may Take Cover behind you.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Gain [Optimized] on all defense rolls against Ranged attacks. You act as Cover 4 in your zone — allies may Take Cover behind you.

abilities.shorthand: Stance: [Optimized] vs Ranged defense. Masterful: act as Cover 2. Legendary: act as Cover 4. Allies must Take Cover. You don't benefit from own Cover. Breaks on zone change.

abilities.name: Circle of Shelter
abilities.rule:
While in Resilience Stance, when an ally in your zone who is benefitting from Cover you provide fails a defense against a Ranged attack, you may spend 1 Edge to intercept the shot. You sense the killing bolt before it lands. You become the new target and must Endure.

You choose: retain [Optimized] on this Endure roll, but the stance breaks. Or ignore [Optimized] on this Endure roll, and the stance holds.
abilities.shorthand: While in Resilience Stance, spend 1 Edge to intercept Ranged attack on ally using your Cover. Endure the shot: keep [Optimized] but break stance, or forgo [Optimized] and stance holds.

abilities.name: March of the Mynock
abilities.rule:
While maintaining Resilience Stance:

You may spend your Action pip to make the Move maneuver [Weakened] without breaking the stance or losing your zone effects. When you do, all allies in your zone may spend their Exploit pip to move with you. This movement does not trigger Openings from Ranged attackers.

You may spend Edge instead of your Exploit pip to Reflect (Tier 2).
abilities.shorthand: While in Resilience Stance: Action pip for [Weakened] Move without breaking stance, allies may spend Exploit to move with you (no Ranged Openings). Edge can replace Exploit pip for Reflect.

name: The Skirmisher
description: The Skirmisher does not hold ground — she takes it, uses it, and abandons it before the enemy can respond. Light weapons, constant movement, and an instinct for angles that turns every battlefield into a series of one-sided engagements. She fights the way a river erodes stone: not by force, but by refusing to be where the resistance is. The enemy shoots at where she was. She is already somewhere else, and she is already shooting back.
fluff: Every army has its line infantry. Every rebellion has its skirmishers. They are the ones who hit the supply convoy and vanish into the tree line. The ones who fire from a rooftop, relocate before the return volley, and are three blocks away before the stormtroopers kick down the door. The Empire calls them insurgents. The Rebellion calls them force multipliers. On the Outer Rim, where the war is fought in alleyways and cantina back rooms, they are simply the ones who survive — because they never stop moving and they never fight fair.

abilities.name: Running Battle
abilities.rule:
While carrying only light weapons (blaster pistols, light melee weapons, thrown weapons — no rifles, heavy weapons, or two-handed weapons), you may use Tactics (Wits) in place of the weapon's normal discipline (Ranged or Melee) for attack rolls, but only if you have moved since the start of your turn.

Your combat effectiveness comes from positioning, not marksmanship. The shot is accurate because you chose the right angle, the strike lands because you found the gap in their guard while they were still tracking your last position. Stand still and you are just another fighter. Move and you fight with your mind.
abilities.shorthand: Tactics (Wits) replaces weapon discipline for light weapon attacks if you have moved since the start of your turn.

abilities.name: Reactionary Movement
abilities.rule:
When an enemy enters your zone, you may declare an Opening and spend your Exploit pip to immediately take a Move maneuver instead of making an attack. You read the approach and you are already gone — by the time they arrive at your position, you are not in it.

This uses your Exploit pip for the round. You cannot use Reactionary Movement and also make an offensive Exploit this round — you chose escape over damage. If you are currently Engaged with other enemies, the normal disengagement rules apply to your Move (you may present Openings to enemies you are already Engaged with).
abilities.shorthand: When enemy enters your zone, spend Exploit pip to Move instead of attack.

abilities.name: Evasive Footwork
abilities.rule:
If you have moved since the start of your turn, your Dodge defense rolls are [Optimized] (Control die steps up) against ranged, non-area-of-effect attacks. Single-target ranged fire cannot track you — you are a moving target and every shot arrives where you were, not where you are.

This does not apply to area-of-effect weapons (grenades, explosives, suppressive fire), melee attacks, or Force powers. You cannot dodge a blast radius by running. The protection requires movement — if you have not moved since the start of your turn, your Dodge functions normally.
abilities.shorthand: Dodge is [Optimized] vs ranged non-AoE attacks if you have moved since the start of your turn.

abilities.name: Harrying Strike
abilities.rule: Light weapons only. As part of this maneuver, you Move 1 zone before making the roll — this movement counts for activating Running Battle and Evasive Footwork. Roll Tactics (Wits) vs target's Resolve. You fire to control, not to kill — cutting off angles, herding the target, forcing them where you want them.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening — you committed to the repositioning shot and left yourself exposed.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Disoriented] (immediate).

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Disoriented] (immediate) and is forced to Move 1 zone in a direction you choose.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Disoriented] (tactical duration) and is forced to Move 1 zone in a direction you choose.

abilities.shorthand: Move 1 zone, then Tactics (Wits) vs Resolve. Fleeting: [Disoriented]. Masterful: [Disoriented] + forced Move 1 zone. Legendary: [Disoriented] (tactical) + forced Move 1 zone. Risk: present Opening.

abilities.name: Ghost Protocol
abilities.rule:
Enemies you have inflicted [Disoriented] on cannot target you with attacks while the condition persists. You have scrambled their ability to track you — they know you are out there, but they cannot find you. They can target your allies, take cover, attempt to clear the condition, or act in any other way. They simply cannot aim at the thing that rattled their coordination.

This applies only to enemies you personally inflicted [Disoriented] on. An ally's grenade or a separate source of [Disoriented] does not feed this ability. Area-of-effect attacks that do not require targeting (explosions, suppressive fire zones) can still affect you normally — they are not aimed at you, they are aimed at the ground.
abilities.shorthand: Enemies you inflicted [Disoriented] on cannot target you with attacks while the condition persists.

name: The Splice
description: Slicers talk to machines and machines talk back. The Splice doesn't just use technology — they command it. Through a companion probe droid and a talent for invasive system access, they extend their reach into any networked system, locked terminal, or security grid that makes the mistake of having a physical port.
fluff: Chopper kept the Ghost flying with attitude, bad wiring, and creative profanity. R2-D2 sliced Star Destroyers from the inside while everyone else was busy getting shot. The best slicers in the galaxy aren't the ones who sit behind terminals — they're the ones who send something small, fast, and expendable to do the job while they watch from a safe distance. Your droid isn't your pet. It's your hands where your hands can't reach.

abilities.name: Companion Droid
abilities.rule:
You begin play with a small hovering probe droid — a scomp-link equipped utility platform with a basic toolkit, sensor suite, and holoprojector. The droid occupies your zone and can move up to 3 zones from you. It acts on your initiative using your pips — commanding it costs the same as acting yourself.

The droid has no independent will. It does exactly what you tell it and nothing else. When the droid is targeted by an attack or hazard, you defend for it using Tech (Wits) as both Control and Power. If the defense fails, the droid enters [Shut Down] and must be repaired with a Tech action during a Short Rest.

The droid can interact with physical systems (plug into terminals, activate switches, carry small objects) but cannot attack, intimidate, or perform social actions. It is a tool, not a combatant.
abilities.shorthand: Companion droid can Interact with physical systems (terminals, switches, carry objects). Modes: Scout, Combat, Fortify.

abilities.name: Direct
abilities.rule:
When your droid or any system you have physically accessed via scomp-link is under your control, you may spend a Maneuver pip to issue a Direct command. The controlled system provides its own Power die (determined by the GM based on the system's capability), and you use Tech (Wits) as your Control die.

The system's capabilities determine what Direct can do — a turret can fire, a door can lock, a security camera can pan, a ventilation system can vent atmosphere. You cannot Direct a system to do something it was not built to do. No turret means no attack. No lock means no seal.

Direct requires maintained physical connection (scomp-link) or active control via Companion Droid. Breaking the connection ends control immediately.
abilities.shorthand: Spend Maneuver to direct droid/sliced system to Interact. Tech (Wits) for complex commands.

abilities.name: Reconfigure
abilities.rule:
During a Short Rest, you may reconfigure your Companion Droid into one of three operational modes. The mode persists until the next Short Rest. Choose one:

**Scout Mode:** The droid's operational range extends to unlimited distance (as long as comm signal is maintained). It gains enhanced sensors — you may Assess through the droid using Tech (Wits) at no range penalty. The droid cannot interact with systems in this mode — it observes only.

**Combat Mode:** The droid gains a light blaster mount. It may make Ranged attacks using your Tech (Wits) as Control and a D6 Power die. Range: 2 zones. The droid's operational range remains 5 zones.

**Fortify Mode:** The droid projects a localized energy shield. One ally in the droid's zone gains [Buffered 2] at the start of each round. The droid's operational range is reduced to 2 zones and it cannot interact with systems while maintaining the shield.

effect.label: Scout Mode
effect.description: Unlimited range, enhanced sensors, Assess through droid at no penalty. Observation only — no system interaction.

effect.label: Combat Mode
effect.description: Light blaster mount (D6 Power, 2-zone range). Attacks use Tech (Wits). Operational range 5 zones.

effect.label: Fortify Mode
effect.description: Energy shield grants [Buffered 2] to one ally in droid zone each round. Operational range reduced to 2 zones, no system interaction.

abilities.name: Autonomous Protocol
abilities.rule:
Your Companion Droid gains limited autonomous behavior during Challenge scenes. It may take one independent Maneuver pip per round without costing you a pip — the droid acts on pre-programmed subroutines you've drilled into it.

The autonomous Maneuver must be consistent with the droid's current mode: Scout droid repositions or scans, Combat droid repositions or takes cover, Fortify droid repositions the shield. The droid still cannot take Actions independently — complex commands still require your Direct.
abilities.shorthand: Droid gets 1 free Maneuver/round (autonomous). Can Interact independently in current mode.

abilities.name: Parallel Processing
abilities.rule:
Once per Act, you may activate Parallel Processing. For the remainder of the current Challenge scene, your Companion Droid gains a full independent combat turn — its own Action and Maneuver pips, acting on your initiative. You command both yourself and the droid simultaneously, splitting your attention across two bodies.

The droid uses Tech (Wits) for all rolls during Parallel Processing. Its capabilities are still limited by its current Reconfigure mode.

If you are rendered unconscious while Parallel Processing is active, the droid does not shut down. It continues operating on the last standing orders you gave it — a final act of programming foresight that keeps your tools in the fight even when you can't be.
